title: Himatanthus tarapotensis
taxonomy: Himatanthus tarapotensis is classified as a species of plant in the Apocynaceae family. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family, and it includes over 5,000 species of plants. Himatanthus tarapotensis is a woody vine native to the Amazon rainforest, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Within the Apocynaceae family, Himatanthus tarapotensis is placed in the tribe Plumerieae, which includes other species of plants with similar growth habits and flower structures. The exact classification of Himatanthus tarapotensis is as follows: Himatanthus tarapotensis is a species of the genus Himatanthus, the family Apocynaceae, the tribe Plumerieae, and the order Gentianales.

intro: Himatanthus tarapotensis, also known as Ayahuasca branca, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The health benefits of Himatanthus tarapotensis include its potential to act as a natural anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, depression, and other conditions.
